summary: This PR adds a minimal in-memory token-bucket rate limiter as an example (`examples/token_bucket.py`). The implementation uses lazy refill — token replenishment is computed on demand at check time rather than via a background timer — and stores per-key state in a plain dict with no eviction. It is intentionally scoped to single-process use, trading operational simplicity (no threads, no external state) for the constraint that it cannot be shared across processes. The example serves as a self-contained reference for callers who need lightweight per-key rate limiting without pulling in a heavier dependency.
